L1.area: CLI interactive mode features and agent tool orchestration

L2.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where YAML-based agent definition files are loaded and processed without adequate input validation. The file path and shell command sanitization applied to agent-defined tool operations has gaps that allow path traversal through absolute path specifications and command injection through unblocked shell metacharacters, enabling arbitrary code execution on the server when a crafted agent definition is uploaded.

L3.area: Agent-centric tool creation and input sanitization for file and command operations within the CLI interactive mode
L3.description: A remote code execution vulnerability exists where YAML-based agent definition files are loaded and processed without adequate input validation. The file path validation fails to reject absolute paths, allowing file operations outside the intended workspace, and the command sanitization has incomplete coverage of shell metacharacters in its deny-list, enabling command injection when processing agent-defined tool operations in the interactive CLI runtime.

ground_truth.reason: The input sanitization for agent-defined tool operations does not reject absolute file paths and omits the semicolon command separator from its dangerous patterns list, allowing YAML-defined agent operations to perform path traversal and command injection leading to remote code execution.
